subtitle: Across Monsoon Seas, Under a Single Guild Banner

panels.shot: sweeping low-angle hero wide from a ship's prow
panels.action: Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant stands at the carved prow of a large Tamil ocean-going ship, hair and cloth whipped by the wind, as the vessel cuts across a foaming monsoon sea toward a distant tropical coastline.

dialogue.text: In the age of the Colas, Tamil merchant guilds rode the monsoon winds, binding the Indian Ocean with ships, contracts, and trust.

i18n.hi: चोलाओं के दौर में तमिल संघ मानसून संग उड़ते, जहाज़, सौदे और भरोसे से हिंद महासागर को बाँधते थे

panels.shot: wide establishing shot of the Coromandel coast shipyard
panels.action: On a bright Coromandel morning, Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant oversees carpenters and caulkers shaping a massive teak hull on a sandy shipyard, with stacked bales of cloth and jars of oil nearby and the Chola coast stretching behind.

dialogue.text: Along the Coromandel shore, under the Colas, shipyards hummed. Timber, rope, and tar turned into ocean-faring hulls, ready to chase the rains eastward.

i18n.hi: कोरोमंडल तट पर चोलाओं के नीचे शिपयार्ड गूँजते; लकड़ी, रस्सी, गोंद से समुंदर चीरने वाले जहाज़ बनते, बारिशों के पीछे पूरब को दौड़ने को

panels.shot: medium shot over table with guild members, contracts and seals
panels.action: Inside a pillared coastal guild hall lit by oil lamps, Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant leans over a low table strewn with palm-leaf documents and metal seals, as fellow merchants gesture to routes marked on a cloth map.

dialogue.text: Here sat the Ainnurruvar, the Five Hundred, and the Manigramam. Guilds of merchants, not kings, planned voyages, weighed risk, and bound each other with oaths.

i18n.hi: यहीं बैठते थे ऐन्नुरुवर, पाँच सौ, और मणिग्रामम; राजा नहीं, व्यापारी संघ सफ़र तय करते, जोखिम तौलते, क़समों से एक-दूसरे को बाँधते

panels.shot: close-up on hands sealing a palm-leaf charter
panels.action: Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant presses an ornate ring-seal into a lump of soft clay on a rolled palm-leaf charter, while another merchant steadies the document, their fingers stained with ink and oil.

dialogue.text: No printing presses, no paper. Palm-leaf charters and metal seals carried the weight of their promises from port to port.

i18n.hi: न छापाखाने, न कागज़; ताड़पत्र फ़रमान और धातु की मुहरें ही बंदर से बंदर तक उनके वादों का वज़न उठातीं

panels.shot: wide shot of cargo being loaded at dusk
panels.action: At dusk on the harbour, dockworkers haul bales of fine cotton, bundles of pepper, gleaming bronze vessels, and iron bars up a plank into the hold, as Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant counts quietly with a tally stick.

dialogue.text: Their wealth was not only coin. Cloth, spices, metalwork, and the credit of their name filled the holds heavier than any stone ballast.

i18n.hi: उनकी दौलत सिर्फ़ सिक्के नहीं; कपड़ा, मसाले, धातु-कला और उनके नाम की साख जहाज़ों को पत्थर के गट्टों से भारी कर देती

panels.shot: epic bird's-eye of ships leaving the Coromandel coast
panels.action: From high above, a small convoy of Tamil ships spreads its sails and slips away from the bustling Coromandel coast, leaving behind temple towers and palm-fringed villages as clouds of seabirds circle overhead.

dialogue.text: When the monsoon turned, they followed it. From Kaveri delta ports, Chola ships fanned out toward Sri Lanka, the Malay Peninsula, and beyond.

i18n.hi: मानसून पलटता, तो वे भी; कावेरी डेल्टा के घाटों से चोल जहाज़ श्रीलंका, मलय प्रायद्वीप और उससे आगे फैल जाते

panels.shot: dramatic low-angle on deck in stormy seas
panels.action: On a heaving deck under dark, wind-torn clouds, Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant grips a wet rope and shouts orders, as rain lashes the crew and the ship rides a towering swell.

dialogue.text: The Indian Ocean gave and it tested. Waves rose like walls, masts groaned, but guild ships learned to read the sky and survive the cycle of winds.

i18n.hi: भारतीय महासागर देता भी, आज़माता भी; लहरें दीवार-सी उठतीं, मस्तूल कराहते, पर संघ के जहाज़ आसमान पढ़ना सीख गए, हवाओं के चक्र में टिकना सीख गए

panels.shot: wide shot of a Southeast Asian port with mixed architecture
panels.action: Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant stands at the rail as the ship approaches a busy Sumatran port, where wooden jetties, foreign-style roofs, and a mix of Indian and local structures crowd a lush shoreline dotted with other anchored vessels.

dialogue.text: Beyond Lanka lay the Straits and Sumatra. Here, strange tongues mingled with Tamil, and temples shared the skyline with shrines of other gods.

i18n.hi: लंका के पार जलडमरूमध्य और सुमात्रा; यहाँ अजीब ज़बानें तमिल से घुलतीं, और मंदिरों के साथ पराये देवताओं की वेदियाँ भी skyline बाँटतीं

panels.shot: medium shot of Barus shoreline with inscription stone being set
panels.action: On a sandy clearing at Barus, local workers raise a freshly carved stone slab upright, while Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant watches solemnly, hands folded, as Tamil craftsmen smooth the still-rough inscription surface.

dialogue.text: At Barus on Sumatra’s coast, they left more than cargo. In Saka 1010, a Tamil stone rose, carved with the name of the Ainnurruvar for all to see.

i18n.hi: सुमात्रा के तट पर बारुस में वे सिर्फ़ माल नहीं छोड़ते; शaka 1010 में एक तमिल शिला उठी, जिस पर ऐन्नुरुवर का नाम सबके लिए उकेरा गया

panels.shot: extreme close-up on the Lobu Tua inscription surface
panels.action: An extreme close-up shows Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant's weathered hand gently touching the crisp Tamil letters on the Lobu Tua stone, the incised lines catching the tropical light.

dialogue.text: The Lobu Tua inscription spoke of guilds and agreements. It proved that Tamil merchants stood here, far from home, yet bound by familiar rules.

i18n.hi: लोबू तुआ अभिलेख ने संघों और संधियों की बातें कीं; उसने साबित किया कि तमिल व्यापारी यहाँ खड़े थे, घर से दूर, पर अपने ही नियमों से बँधे

panels.shot: medium shot of cross-cultural trade negotiation
panels.action: Inside a shaded pavilion in the Sumatran port, Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant sits cross-legged opposite a local Southeast Asian chief and a Middle Eastern trader, all three surrounded by small heaps of goods as they gesture calmly in negotiation.

dialogue.speaker: Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant
dialogue.text: We trade in trust; our guild stands behind every measure and weight.

i18n.hi: हम भरोसा बेचते हैं; हर तौल, हर माप के पीछे हमारा संघ खड़ा है

panels.shot: wide shot inside a shared warehouse
panels.action: In a large wooden warehouse, Tamil workers stack textiles beside jars of aromatic resins, while local porters move bundles of forest products; Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant oversees, sharing a brief nod with a local official at the doorway.

dialogue.text: Warehouse by warehouse, they built routines. Shared storage, fixed dues, agreed rules of conduct. So the guild’s name became a promise in distant markets.

i18n.hi: गोदाम पर गोदाम, उन्होंने रिवाज़ गढ़े; साझा भंडार, तय नज़राना, मान्य आचार–इसी से संघ का नाम दूर देशों में वादा बन गया

panels.shot: transition shot of a map-style montage
panels.action: A stylized parchment map fills the frame, with tiny painted ships tracing dotted routes from the Coromandel coast to Lanka, Srivijaya ports, the Gulf of Thailand, and up to Chinese harbours, while a small figure of Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant stands at the bottom edge.

dialogue.text: From inscription to inscription, the trail runs: Sri Lanka, the Malay world, the shores of the China Sea, all touched by Tamil guild traffic.

i18n.hi: शिलालेख से शिलालेख तक लकीर चलती है; श्रीलंका, मलय जगत, चीन सागर के तट–सब तमिल संघों की आवाजाही से छुए गए

panels.shot: wide shot of a bustling Chinese harbour in Quanzhou-style setting
panels.action: Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant disembarks at a vast Chinese harbour, where towering junks loom over Tamil vessels, warehouses line the quay, and Chinese officials in robes walk past stalls selling Chola-region goods.

dialogue.text: Chinese chroniclers like Zhao Rugua wrote of Chu-lien, the Chola land, its cloth and aromatics. Even in Quanzhou’s harbours, their region’s name weighed on trade lists.

i18n.hi: झाओ रु–गुआ जैसे चीनी इतिहासकार चू–लियेन, यानी चोल देश, उसके कपड़े और सुगंध लिखते; क्वानझो के बंदरगाहों में भी उनके देश का नाम भाव-सूची पर भारी पड़ता

panels.shot: medium shot of merchant examining Chinese account scroll
panels.action: Inside a Chinese counting house, Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant peers curiously at a Chinese clerk’s scroll listing foreign lands, while the clerk points to a line with brush in hand, shelves of goods rising behind them.

dialogue.text: Where Zhao Rugua saw names, the guild saw opportunity. Lists of far-off lands became reminders that their contracts reached the edge of the known sea.

i18n.hi: जहाँ झाओ रु–गुआ को सिर्फ़ नाम दिखे, वहाँ संघ को मौक़े दिखे; दूर देशों की सूचियाँ उन्हें याद दिलातीं कि उनके सौदे ज्ञात समुंदर की हद तक पहुँच चुके हैं

panels.shot: intimate medium shot of guild meeting in a foreign temple
panels.action: In a small Tamil-style shrine nestled within a foreign city, Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant meets with a circle of fellow guild members, a simple oil lamp between them, as they quietly discuss future voyages.

dialogue.text: Wherever they settled, they carved out corners of home. A shrine, a hall, a place to renew vows—so that Chola coast customs could endure among alien streets.

i18n.hi: जहाँ भी बसे, घर का एक कोना गढ़ा; एक देवालय, सभा–मंडप, व्रत दोहराने की जगह–ताकि चोल तट की रीतें परायी गलियों में भी जिंदा रहें

panels.shot: wide shot of returning ships reaching the Tamil coast
panels.action: Several weathered ships glide back toward the familiar Tamil coast at sunset, their hulls lower in the water with cargo, as Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant smiles faintly, hands resting on the rail, temple gopurams rising ahead.

dialogue.text: Season after season, they returned. Profits fed temples, towns, and kings’ ambitions, but the guild’s strength lay in its long memory of routes and debts.

i18n.hi: मौसम दर मौसम वे लौटते; मुनाफ़ा मंदिरों, नगरों, राजाओं के सपनों में जाता, पर संघ की असली ताक़त रास्तों और उधारों की पुरानी याद में थी

panels.shot: close-up on the merchant teaching a younger apprentice
panels.action: In the same coastal guild hall, now older, Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant points to a route on a map while a young apprentice listens intently, palm-leaf stylus in hand, the hall’s pillars darkened by years of incense.

dialogue.speaker: Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant
dialogue.text: Ships rot, storms come. What endures is the trust in our name.

i18n.hi: जहाज़ गलते, आँधियाँ टूटती हैं; जो टिकता है, वह है हमारे नाम पर रखा भरोसा

panels.shot: slow push-in on the Lobu Tua stone centuries later in a quiet grove
panels.action: Centuries later, the Lobu Tua stone stands weathered yet upright in a quiet Sumatran grove, dappled with sunlight, as a faint silhouette of Ainnurruvar Sea-Merchant’s figure seems to hover like a memory behind it.

dialogue.text: Today the ships are gone, but their inscriptions remain—silent witnesses that trust, shared rules, and patient trade once tied the Indian Ocean into a single, humming world.

i18n.hi: आज जहाज़ नहीं, पर उनके अभिलेख हैं; चुप साक्षी कि कभी भरोसे, साझा नियमों और धीरज भरे व्यापार ने हिंद महासागर को एक गूँजती, जुड़ी हुई दुनिया में बाँध दिया था

title_i18n.en: Tamil Merchant Guilds Sail the Indian Ocean
title_i18n.hi: तमिल व्यापारी संघ भारतीय महासागर पर छा गए

subtitle_i18n.en: Across Monsoon Seas, Under a Single Guild Banner
subtitle_i18n.hi: एक ही संघ के झंडे तले, मानसून सागर पार
